workflow.name: Puber Feature Delivery
workflow.description: Plan, implement, audit, fix, optionally smoke-test, and conservatively clean up a Puber Android feature.

transition_groups.description: Start Puber feature planning from the task body.
Plan is ready; user approval required before implementation.
Planning is blocked by missing context, access, or product input.
More implementation steps remain; continue in the same session.
All implementation steps are complete; run read-only audit.
Implementation is blocked by missing input, access, or unsafe state.
Audit found issues; user approval required before fixes.
Audit is acceptable and a focused device smoke test is useful.
Audit is acceptable and no smoke test is needed; run mandatory compliance review before cleanup.
Audit cannot continue due to missing access, context, or verification.
Fixing is blocked by missing input, unsafe state, or verification failure.
Smoke test passed or is accepted; run mandatory compliance review before cleanup.
Smoke test found implementation issues; user approval required before fixes.
Smoke test is blocked by missing device, MCP, build, or credentials.
Cleanup report is ready; finish the task.
Compliance review found no violations; run conservative cleanup.
Compliance review found violations; user approval required before fixes.
Compliance review is blocked by missing or contradictory required sources.

edges.prompt_template:
Run the Puber feature planning flow for this Kent task.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first, then use .kent/commands/feature-start.md as the project feature planning command. Treat the task title/body as the feature arguments and source context. Resolve or create an explicit .todo/<feature> workspace; do not infer or create .todo/.current.

Gather available Figma/spec/source context through project-local adapters only when present. Produce .todo/<feature>/spec.md as needed and .todo/<feature>/plan.md. Do not implement production code.

Complete with transition implement when the plan is ready for user approval and provide workspace_path plus plan_path. Complete with blocked if required source context or product input is unavailable, and provide blocker_reason in the task language when practical.

Implement the approved Puber feature plan.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first. Use .kent/commands/feature-implement.md with explicit workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}} and plan {{.Params.plan_path}}. Do not infer .todo/.current.

Implement one next unchecked ready step or one ready parallel group. For parallel groups, delegate to project-local orchestration with: kent run --agent=feature-orchestrator --workspace "$PWD" "<bounded prompt containing workspace_path, plan_path, selected steps, file boundaries, and compile command>". Use kent run --agent=implementation-worker for bounded slices and kent run --agent=build-doctor for noisy compile failures.

Verify with the narrowest relevant Gradle/test command, using ./tools/agentw in .kent/worktrees and ./gradlew in the main checkout. Update plan.md and meta.json progress.

If more unchecked implementation steps remain, complete with transition continue_implementation and provide workspace_path plus plan_path again. If all implementation steps are complete, complete with transition audit and provide workspace_path, plan_path, and verification_report. Complete with blocked on unresolved blockers and provide blocker_reason.

parameters.description: Path to the explicit .todo/<feature> workspace created by planning.
Path to the generated plan.md inside the feature workspace.
Short human-readable blocker explanation. Use the same language as the task/user request when practical; include the missing access/input/check and next action needed.

edges.prompt_template:
Continue the same Puber feature implementation.

Read .kent/project-contract.md, then use .kent/commands/feature-implement.md with explicit workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}} and plan {{.Params.plan_path}}. Do not infer .todo/.current.

Select the next unchecked ready step or ready parallel group, implement it, verify it, and update plan.md/meta.json. Use project-local delegated agents only for bounded work: feature-orchestrator, implementation-worker, and build-doctor.

Complete with continue_implementation and provide workspace_path plus plan_path if more steps remain. Complete with audit and provide workspace_path, plan_path, and verification_report when all implementation steps are complete. Complete with blocked and blocker_reason if blocked.

parameters.description: Path to the explicit .todo/<feature> workspace.
Path to plan.md inside the feature workspace.

edges.prompt_template:
Run a read-only Puber feature audit.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first. Use .kent/commands/feature-audit.md with explicit workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}} and plan {{.Params.plan_path}}. Do not infer .todo/.current.

Review implementation against spec, optional design artifacts, plan, Puber architecture in AGENTS.md, Compose quality, API/domain contracts, and verification coverage. Delegate broad read-only review when useful with kent run --agent=quality-reviewer --workspace "$PWD" "<bounded review prompt>". Use compose-reviewer and domain-model-reviewer for focused read-only review when applicable.

Write/update an audit report under the feature workspace. Complete with needs_changes if fixes are required and provide workspace_path plus audit_report. Complete with smoke if implementation is acceptable and device smoke is useful, providing workspace_path plus audit_report. Complete with done if no smoke is needed, providing workspace_path plus audit_report. Complete with blocked and blocker_reason if verification cannot continue.

parameters.description: Path to the explicit .todo/<feature> workspace.
Path to plan.md inside the feature workspace.
Commands/tests run and their results, or path to a verification report.
Short human-readable blocker explanation. Use the same language as the task/user request when practical; include the missing access/input/check and next action needed.

edges.prompt_template:
Apply approved fixes for the Puber feature audit.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first. Use .kent/commands/feature-fix.md with explicit workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}} and audit report {{.Params.audit_report}}, or apply the smallest safe manual fixes described in the audit report. Do not infer .todo/.current.

Use kent run --agent=implementation-worker for bounded fix slices and kent run --agent=build-doctor for compile diagnostics when useful. Keep the fix scope narrow, verify locally, update the audit report, then complete with transition audit and provide workspace_path plus audit_report. Complete with blocked and blocker_reason if blocked.

parameters.description: Path to the explicit .todo/<feature> workspace.
Path to the audit report for the feature workspace.

edges.prompt_template:
Run a focused Puber device smoke test.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first. Use .kent/commands/smoke-test.md for workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}} and audit report {{.Params.audit_report}}. Install the freshly built devDebug APK before device testing unless the task explicitly says the intended build is already running. Use Mobile MCP through .kent/adapters/mcp wrappers, save useful artifacts under build/test-artifacts or the feature workspace, and report issues.

Complete with done if smoke passes. Complete with needs_changes if smoke finds implementation issues and provide workspace_path, audit_report, and smoke_report. Complete with blocked and blocker_reason if device/MCP/build access is unavailable.

parameters.description: Path to the explicit .todo/<feature> workspace.
Path to the audit report for the feature workspace.

edges.prompt_template:
Run mandatory Puber Compliance Review before cleanup.

Read .kent/commands/compliance-review.md, .kent/project-contract.md, and AGENTS.md first. Reviewed scope: completed feature implementation in workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}} after audit {{.Params.audit_report}}. Review only compliance with task requirements, plan/design/specs, AGENTS.md, and workflow contract. Do not edit files.

Complete with done and provide compliance_report when no compliance violations are found. Complete with needs_changes and provide workspace_path plus compliance_report when fixes are required. Complete with blocked and provide blocker_reason if required sources are missing or contradictory.

parameters.description: Path to the explicit .todo/<feature> workspace.
Path to the audit report for the feature workspace.
Short human-readable blocker explanation. Use the same language as the task/user request when practical; include the missing access/input/check and next action needed.

edges.prompt_template:
Re-run the Puber feature audit after fixes.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first. Re-check workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}} with audit report {{.Params.audit_report}}. Focus on previous findings plus regressions from the fix pass. Do not infer .todo/.current.

Complete with needs_changes, smoke, done, or blocked. Provide workspace_path plus audit_report when continuing, or blocker_reason when blocked.

parameters.description: Path to the explicit .todo/<feature> workspace.
Path to the audit report after fixes or concise fix summary.
Short human-readable blocker explanation. Use the same language as the task/user request when practical; include the missing access/input/check and next action needed.

edges.prompt_template:
Run mandatory Puber Compliance Review before cleanup.

Read .kent/commands/compliance-review.md, .kent/project-contract.md, and AGENTS.md first. Reviewed scope: completed feature implementation in workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}} after audit/smoke context {{.Params.audit_report}}. Review only compliance with task requirements, plan/design/specs, AGENTS.md, and workflow contract. Do not edit files.

Complete with done and provide compliance_report when no compliance violations are found. Complete with needs_changes and provide workspace_path plus compliance_report when fixes are required. Complete with blocked and provide blocker_reason if required sources are missing or contradictory.

parameters.description: Path to the explicit .todo/<feature> workspace.
Path to the audit report for the feature workspace.

edges.prompt_template:
Fix approved issues found during Puber smoke testing.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first. Use workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}}, audit report {{.Params.audit_report}}, and smoke report {{.Params.smoke_report}}. Keep fixes narrow, verify locally, update the audit report, then complete with audit and provide workspace_path plus audit_report. Complete with blocked and blocker_reason if blocked.

parameters.description: Path to the explicit .todo/<feature> workspace.
Path to the audit report for the feature workspace.
Smoke test issue report with artifacts or concise summary.
Short human-readable blocker explanation. Use the same language as the task/user request when practical; include the missing access/input/check and next action needed.
Short human-readable cleanup result. Include removed/skipped/safe-to-remove worktree and branch names, or the reason cleanup could not run.

edges.prompt_template:
Run conservative Puber task cleanup after compliance passed.

Read .kent/project-contract.md first, then use .kent/commands/cleanup-task.md. Compliance result: {{.Params.compliance_report}}. Only clean project-local Kent worktrees under .kent/worktrees/ when safe. Do not delete external/global worktrees or unmerged user changes. Complete with done and provide cleanup_report.

parameters.description: Compliance review report, including rule sources checked and any findings.

edges.prompt_template:
Fix Puber feature compliance violations only.

Read .kent/project-contract.md and .kent/commands/compliance-review.md first. Apply only the minimum changes required by compliance findings in {{.Params.compliance_report}} for workspace {{.Params.workspace_path}}. Verify narrowly, then complete with audit and provide workspace_path plus audit_report. Complete with blocked and provide blocker_reason if unsafe or missing input.

parameters.description: Path to the explicit .todo/<feature> workspace.
Compliance review report, including rule sources checked and any findings.
Why compliance review cannot complete, in the task language when practical.
